articles.title: THE ELEVENLABS AI MUSIC GENERATOR TURNS YOUR IDEAS INTO 3-MINUTE SONGS
articles.summary: ElevenMusic is an iOS app that generates 3-minute songs from text prompts, allowing users to specify mood, length, and lyrical content. The app offers seven free songs daily, with a $9.99/month Pro subscription providing up to 500 tracks per month and additional features. ElevenMusic competes with Google's Lyria 3 Pro and established platforms like Suno and Udio, leveraging ElevenLabs' voice AI expertise as its competitive advantage.

articles.title: IPHONE FOLD DIMENSIONS: HERE'S HOW THE FOLDABLE IPHONE SIZES UP NEXT TO THE IPHONE 18 PRO
articles.summary: Leaked dummy units shared by Sonny Dickson suggest Apple's foldable iPhone will be wider and shorter than Pro Max models when closed, but unfold into a ~7.8-inch display with a usable area closer to an iPad mini, emphasizing a better landscape experience despite a potentially awkward closed form. It's expected to feature an almost invisible crease, use Touch ID instead of Face ID due to space constraints, and be under 5 mm thick when unfolded but slightly thicker than current Pro models when folded.

articles.title: “JUSTICE FOR SERIFS!”: MARVEL FANS ROAST NEW WONDER MAN LOGO
articles.summary: Marvel's Wonder Man series has been warmly received overall, but its newly revealed minimalist logo has sparked backlash from fans who see it as a significant downgrade from the original serif design. The updated version, which features a gray gradient impact-style font, is widely criticized as bland, sterile, and lacking the personality that helped define other Marvel series branding. While minimalist logos can be effective, many fans feel this redesign strips away the charm and flair seen in shows like WandaVision and The Punisher, with some even calling for a return to the older, more expressive serif style.

articles.title: THE SELF-HEALING DESIGN SYSTEM
articles.summary: A design system architect built a self-healing AI infrastructure using Claude Code as an orchestration layer, connecting six specialized agents to Figma, GitHub, Storybook, and other tools via MCP. The system automatically detects drift, scores violations, generates fix PRs, and updates documentation — turning every human correction into a learning signal that sharpens future suggestions. Critically, full autonomy is never the goal: a trust-level matrix ensures high-risk changes always require human review, since agents, unlike human experts, don't explain their failures or take ownership of mistakes.

articles.title: VIBE CODING IS ACCELERATING THE EROSION OF DESIGN AUTHORITY
articles.summary: Vibe coding tools like Google Stitch generate interfaces rapidly but produce executable prototypes, not production-ready systems — a distinction the term itself obscures. The industry's cultural shift now demands visible, interactive output before architectural decisions are trusted, effectively replacing invisible reasoning with the appearance of progress. Renaming the practice "vibe prototyping" would be more honest, but the deeper fix requires designers to explicitly separate surface demonstrations from structural decisions.

articles.title: THE INVISIBLE LAYER OF UX MOST DESIGNERS IGNORE
articles.summary: Most designers focus on visual polish, but screen reader users experience interfaces as a linear stream of spoken content, meaning usability depends on proper structure (roles, names, and states) rather than appearance. Many accessibility issues come from missing semantic meaning—like vague labels, misuse of components, or reliance on visual cues. Designers can greatly improve accessibility by thinking beyond visuals, designing with structure in mind, and testing their work with screen readers early in the process.

articles.title: 2D TO 3D RENDERING PLATFORM
articles.summary: Depthflow AI is an AI-powered platform that converts static images into immersive 3D depth-motion visuals.

articles.title: HAND-DRAWN MARKUP FOR SCREENSHOTS
articles.summary: Hndmark turns your shots into authentic work-in-progress with sketchy circles, arrows, and annotations.

articles.title: DRAW ON A SHARED GLOBAL CANVAS
articles.summary: Interlude is a shared canvas app where strangers draw light trails with their fingers that fade within seconds. The app was created as an alternative to accumulation-based digital spaces, offering a temporary, human experience without tracking or turning attention into currency.

articles.title: UX WRITING FOR CONVERSION: HOW MICROCOPY INCREASES FORM SUBMISSIONS AND REDUCES FRICTION
articles.summary: UX writing is the discipline of crafting interface text to reduce user friction and increase conversions, with weak microcopy being the root cause of most form abandonment issues. Effective UX writing focuses on six key areas: field labels, helper text, error messages, calls-to-action, confirmation messages, and privacy notices that build trust and guide users clearly. Strategic microcopy transforms forms from friction points into conversion tools by eliminating user uncertainty and hesitation at each step.

articles.title: YOUR CURRENT SET-UP MAY NOT BE ALIGNING WITH WHERE YOU WANT TO BE
articles.summary: Loss of creative passion is likely caused by burnout driven by fear of failure, anxiety, and misalignment with their work environment, rather than a true loss of creativity. Give yourself space to explore, reassess what inspires you, and gradually rebuild creative energy by aligning your work and habits with your values instead of chasing perfection.

articles.title: THIS PLAYFUL RULER TURNS STRAIGHT LINES INTO WINDING PATHS
articles.summary: Product designer Tomas Kral created the Way ruler, which features winding, looping lines at each centimeter mark instead of straight edges.

articles.title: VISUAL AESTHETICS: WHY YOUR BRAIN DECIDES WHAT'S BEAUTIFUL
articles.summary: Your brain processes beauty through three simultaneous neural systems that analyze visual data, run emotional appraisals, and integrate meaning within milliseconds.
